title: Cleome cornus-africani
intro: Cleome cornutus, also known as African spider flower, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its vibrant purple flowers and sprawling habit. Traditionally, Cleome cornutus has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cleome cornutus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cleome cornutus is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema.
taxonomy: Cleome cornus-africani is classified under the family Cleomaceae. This family belongs to the order Brassicales in the class Magnoliopsida. The Cleomaceae family is a small family of flowering plants native to the Americas and Africa. Cleome cornus-africani is further classified under the order Brassicales due to its similar characteristics to other plants within this order. The classification of Cleome cornus-africani reflects its unique evolutionary history within the Cleomaceae family.
uses: Cleome cornus-africani is used in traditional medicine, primarily for its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The plant is employed to treat various conditions, including skin infections and wounds. Its extracts are also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain. In some cultures, Cleome cornus-africani is used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Additionally, the plant is said to have antifungal properties, helping to combat fungal infections.

parts: Cleome cornus-africani is a plant species native to tropical Africa. Cleome cornus-africani leaves, stems, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
